summary.p: #36. a) AIM, northern shrine; b) upper inscription engraved across two wall sections, the central and the eastern ones, of the ardha-maṇḍapa of the southern façade; c) personally located and read in situ; d) ARE 1924, no. 379; SII 19, no. 308; SII 32, part 2, no. 99; e) 12th regnal year of Kōpparakesarivarman Uttamacōḻa; f) Uttamacōḻa (circa 983 A.D.); g) inscription not read with anyone.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī kopparakesarivammaṟku yāṇṭu Uttamacoḻa ‡ raṟku 12 Āvatu kuṉṟakkūṟṟattu maṉṉupperumpaḻuvūr
devatāṉam Avaṉikkantavva Īśvagṛhattu mahādevarkku vaṭavāy śrī koyilukku ‡ nāvalūr uṭaiyāṉ kaṇṭaṉ ṟevaṭi makaḷ tevaṭi pukaḻaṟaikku Ivaḷ
bharttār akaṉSII 19 and 32 readbharttā cukaṉ. However, I cannot read cu, but ra or some letter resembling the ra (ka or na). kaliyaṉ Araṅkaṉ Iddevarkku I[[ra]]vum pakalum Eriya vai ‡ tta noṉtāviḷakku Oṉṟiṉukku nicatam ney nārāyattāl ce Uḻakkuku vaitta
cāvāmūvāpperāṭu toṇṇūṟṟāṟu Ivvāṭu koṇṭom maṟavaṉ ‡ neṟi maṉṟāṭi muṇṭan caṅkaṉum [tā]ḻi Eṟaṉum periyāṉ caṭaiyaṉum pe
riyāṉ picaṅkaṉum kecaṉ maḻapāṭiyum kavaripu[ra]ttu maṉṟāṭi vaṭukaṉ vai ‡ ykāvaṭikaḷum cuṟaikaṇṇaTheṇṇa are written one above another. Same for the ṇṇa of kaṇṇan in the same line.ṉum centan vaṭukaṉum kaṇṇan Eḻuva ‡ ṉuSII reads [ivaṉai]vo. However, I cannot see any letters after the ṉu.
m Iṉṉey Aṭṭuvomānom Ivvaṉaivo ‡ m Itdhanmam rakṣippar Avanikandhavvapu[[rattu na]]karattār panmāheśvara ‡ rakṣai ||

Fortune! Prosperity! This is the 12th year of Kōpparakesarivarman Uttamacōḻar. To Mahādeva of the shine (gṛhattu) of the Lord (īśva > īśvara) [of] Avaṉikkantavva, a devadāna of Maṉṉupperumpaḻuvūr of Kuṉṟakkūṟṟam, to the holy shrine (śrī kōyilukku) of the northern side (vaṭavāy), for Tēvaṭi Pukaḻaṟai, daughter (makaḷ) of Kaṇṭaṉ Tēvaṭi, lord (uṭaiyāṉ) of Nāvalūr, Akaṉ Kaliyaṉ Araṅkaṉ, husband (bharttār) of her (ivaḷ), gave (vaitta) to burn (eriya) night and day (iravum pakalum) to this god (iddevarkku); he endowed (vaitta) for one uḻakku and one cey (ce > cey ?) by the nārāyam measure (nārāyattāl) of ghee (ney) everyday (nicatam) for one perpetual lamp (noṉtāviḷakku oṉṟiṉukku > nontāviḷakku oṉṟiṉukku), ninety-six (toṇṇūṟṟāṟu) undying and non-ageing great goats (cāvāmūvāpperāṭu). Having taken (koṇṭōm) these goats (ivvāṭu), we the shepherds (maṉṟāṭi) of Maṟavaṉeṟi, Muṇṭan Caṅkaṉ, Tāḻi Eṟaṉ, Periyāṉ Caṭaiyāṉ, Periyāṉ Picaṅkaṉ, Kecaṉ Maḻapāṭi, and the shepherds (maṉṟāṭi) of Kavaripuram Vaṭukaṉ Vaiykāvaṭikaḷ, Cuṟaikaṇṇaṉ, Cēntaṉ Vaṭukaṉ, Kaṇṇan Eḻuvaṉ, we are those (ivvaṉaivōm) who will supply (aṭṭuvōmānōm) this ghee (iṉṉey). The Nagarattārs of Avanikandhavvapuram will protect (rakṣippar) this donation (itdhanmam). This is under the protection of the Panmāheśvaras.
Edited and translated in , based on autopsy and photographs (by Valérie Gillet, 2016-2018).
This digital edition by Valérie Gillet and Emmanuel Francis.
